summary: Matched and improved several camera-module functions in src/melee/cm, replacing placeholder stubs for Camera_8002CB0C, Camera_8002CDDC, Camera_8002D85C, and Camera_80030E44 with C implementations, while also tightening camera.h prototypes and doing minor matching/formatting adjustments in nearby functions. The work is centered on pause/free-camera subject selection, camera transform fallback handling, FOV/interest/position smoothing, stage camera bounds, and ground/player validity checks. Review feedback focused on keeping static declarations private unless moved properly to camera.h and on running clang-format.
what_changed: Added #include "gr/grlib.h" to camera.c to support Camera_80030E44's calls to grLib_801C9CEC.

Implemented Camera_8002CB0C(CameraBounds* bounds), handling camera input stick thresholds, zoom direction from input bitfields, cycling the camera subject slot via Camera_8002BA00, resolving player or stage camera targets, rejecting invalid or disallowed targets, initializing pause camera vectors, and dispatching to Camera_8002BAA8 / Camera_8002BD88.

Implemented Camera_8002CDDC(void* unused), including transform setup, current pause-camera subject validation, fallback behavior when the subject is invalid, duplicate handling for transform and transform_copy, CameraBounds recalculation, and ground Z-distance checks using Ground_801C57A4 and ftLib_80086644.

Implemented Camera_8002D85C(void* unused), including subject validation, smooth target-interest interpolation, target FOV smoothing, distance calculation from subject size and tanf, pitch/yaw clamping against Stage_GetCamAngleRadians* limits, target-position computation with sinf/cosf, and a larger fallback path using bounds, transform_copy, stage track smoothing, and rolling camera-bound offset averaging.

Implemented Camera_80030E44(enum_t arg0, Vec3* arg1), registering/initializing camera-related ground objects with grLib_801C9CEC, setting camera->_8C state values, and filling empty camera->_B0 entries with either the provided Vec3 or zero vector.

Updated camera.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to more precise signatures, including Camera_8002C5B4(void*), Camera_8002CB0C(CameraBounds*), Camera_8002CDDC(void* unused), Camera_8002D318(void* unused), Camera_8002D85C(void* unused), and Camera_8002E234(void).
Made small local matching/formatting changes in Camera_8002E948 and Camera_8002FEEC, including function-pointer cast spacing, local variable ordering, and PAD_STACK size adjustment.

A proposed change to expose cm_80453004 from camera.static.h was challenged in review; the review suggested moving the declaration to camera.h if external access was needed, otherwise removing the change.
smart_moves: Kept many control-flow shapes close to likely assembly structure by using explicit labels and gotos such as loop_top, loop_body, loop_next, check_valid, fallback_path, check_done, and fallback, which is often necessary for matching complex Metrowerks output.

Used explicit pointer temporaries like slot_ptr, x2C4_ptr, transform, transform_copy, coeff_ptr, base_ptr, pitch_ptr, yaw_ptr, smooth_ptr, and count_ptr to influence register allocation and memory access patterns.
Preserved signed 8-bit slot semantics with s8 pointers/casts for camera subject slots, including special slot values 0xA and 0xB / 10 and 11.
Used 64-bit access to input bitfields, e.g. inputs.x18._u64 & 0x20ULL and inputs.x10._u64 & 0x400ULL, which is a concrete matching tactic noted directly in comments in the added code.
Separated success paths from fallback paths for camera transform update logic, making subject-valid and fallback behavior easier to compare against disassembly.
Duplicated transform and transform_copy handling explicitly instead of over-abstracting, which likely helped match stack/register behavior and original code structure.
Updated public prototypes once function signatures were understood, reducing unknown declarations and improving type propagation for future camera work.
decomp_lessons: For camera decomp, subject slots have important sentinel values: 10/0xA appears to represent a stage camera target path via Stage_UnkSetVec3TCam_Offset, while 11/0xB is treated as invalid/end/fallback in the added functions. Future work should verify but reuse this pattern cautiously.

Camera pause/free-camera logic repeatedly validates Player_GetEntity, ftLib_80086B74, ftLib_8008701C, Camera_8002928C, Camera_80029124, and subject flags before taking the optimized subject-follow path; invalid cases fall back to bounds/transform recalculation.

Stage camera constraints are enforced through Stage_GetCamAngleRadiansUp/Down/Left/Right, Stage_GetPauseCamZPosInit, Stage_GetCamTrackSmooth, and Stage_GetCamBounds*Offset helpers; these APIs are central to matching cm camera behavior.
Matching camera transform code may require keeping transform and transform_copy update blocks separate even when they look structurally identical.
Input-processing code may need exact integer width and union-field access; here button bits are checked through u64 fields rather than narrower fields.
Small stack-layout details matter in this module: Camera_8002FEEC changed local variable ordering and PAD_STACK from 8 to 12, showing that local ordering/padding was part of matching work.

Do not expose static globals from camera.static.h casually. If a symbol must be shared outside camera.c, reviewer guidance was to move the declaration to camera.h instead of just removing static in the static header.
naming_conventions: Function names remain address-based Camera_8002CB0C, Camera_8002CDDC, Camera_8002D85C, Camera_80030E44 because semantic names were not established in the PR evidence.
Header prototypes use explicit parameter names like void* unused where the argument is present for ABI/signature matching but not semantically used.

Local variable names are mostly descriptive matching aids rather than final semantic names: bounds, bounds2, bounds3, transform, transform_copy, slot_ptr, pos_ptr, subject, pitch_ptr, yaw_ptr, tgt_fov_ptr, smooth_ptr, count_ptr.
Camera struct fields remain offset-style names such as x2C4, x2C5, x308, x314, x2D0, x2B8, x2BC, x2C0, _8C, and _B0 where semantics are not fully known.
Global camera/static data names remain address-based, including cm_80452C68 and cm_803BCCA0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use gotos and labels to mirror assembly loops and branch targets in complex camera state machines.
Use explicit signed casts and s8 pointer storage for fields like camera->x2C4 and camera->x2C5 to preserve signed-byte behavior.
Use 64-bit union accesses for controller/input bit tests when the original code appears to load or compare wider values.
Use pointer aliases to struct fields to encourage matching loads/stores and register allocation.
Keep repeated blocks duplicated rather than extracted into helper functions when matching original code generation.
Adjust local declaration order and PAD_STACK size to recover expected stack layout.
Use exact math-library calls and expression shapes for camera transforms: tanf for FOV distance, sinf/cosf for pitch/yaw target position, sqrtf for distance length.

Preserve floating constants and comparison thresholds seen in the diff, including 0.125F stick deadzone, 45.0f and 30.0f Z thresholds, pause eye offset 5.0f/20.0f, and stage slot distance multiplier 3.0f.
review_feedback: Reviewer ribbanya on src/melee/cm/camera.static.h: If cm_80453004 needs to be accessed outside of camera.c, move the declaration to camera.h.
Reviewer ribbanya suggested deleting the attempted change that removed static from cm_80453004 in camera.static.h.
A PR comment requested running clang-format and suggested: clang-format -i -- src/**/**.{c,h}.
Author acknowledged formatting issue: precommit was supposed to run but did not, and they would fix it.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or type Camera_8002CB0C, Camera_8002CDDC, Camera_8002D85C, or Camera_80030E44 semantically.
Check whether cm_80453004 remained static or was later moved to camera.h following review feedback.
Search for later struct improvements to Camera, CameraInputs, CameraTransformState, CameraBounds, and CameraUnkGlobals fields used here.
Compare final matched versions of Camera_8002CDDC and Camera_8002D85C for any removal of gotos or cleanup after matching.
Look for PRs that clarify the meaning of camera slot values 10/0xA and 11/0xB.
